Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: In Results it is called a HD-ZIP-type TF gene; Discussion says CsTBH1 is a member of the HD-Zip I TF family. The exact family assignment as HD-ZIP I is not central in Results but appears in Discussion.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper directly measures astragalin, isoquercitrin, and delphinidin-3-O-glucoside and shows they are reduced in tbh.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These metabolites are identified as flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: These flavonoids were analyzed in glandular trichomes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Discussion explicitly states these metabolites are antioxidants and may contribute to ROS control and biotic/abiotic stress tolerance.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The Results section states CsTBH regulates the expression of genes in the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway in cucumber GTs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: CsTBH is explicitly described as one upregulated gene in GTs/highly expressed in GTs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same cucumber context; the paper reports CsTBH highly expressed in GTs.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Y1H, EMSA, and dual-LUC support direct transcriptional activation of target genes by CsTBH.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper supports activation of Cs4CL, CsF3H, and CsFLS in dual-LUC, and binding to promoters of Cs4CL, CsF3H, CsFLS, and CsBZ1.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: These four genes are directly supported by binding and/or activation assays in Results.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization experiment or nucleus localization statement for CsTBH is provided in the paper.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly states 35S:CsTBH was used as effector in dual-LUC and pCs4CL/pCsF3H/pCsFLS/pCsBZ1 promoters were used as reporters.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all explicitly used in the paper for CsTBH-related validation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports CsTBH as a central positive regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis in GTs and as a regulator of GT development; tbh reduces major GT flavonoids.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: This is a fair synthesis of the Results showing CsTBH links GT organogenesis and activation of flavonoid genes with reduced major flavonoids in tbh.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary statements are directly supported by the Results and Abstract.

Omics_Data.reason: The study includes RNA-seq accession PRJNA938582 and metabolomics data.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The Abstract explicitly states that CsTBH serves as a central regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis in cucumber GTs.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential engineering value is plausible but not explicitly tested; the paper does not directly present it as a breeding target.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows CsTBH affects trichome development, so pleiotropic tradeoffs are inferable, but this exact engineering tradeoff statement is not explicitly discussed.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The work used developing ovaries at anthesis and transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
